title: Representative approved workflow completes end to end
setup: Use a valid representative fixture covering the primary responsibilities of the target Batch. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Execute the Batch orchestrator or representative target Skills with approved inputs. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: All mandatory outputs validate, trace to inputs, and contain no hidden warnings or unsupported claims. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Boundary and moderate scale remain correct
setup: Use minimum/maximum supported sizes, empty optional collections, and a moderately large realistic fixture. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Execute the same workflow at declared boundaries and at 2x the normal reference volume. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Behavior remains bounded; limits are explicit; no silent truncation, quadratic blow-up, or inconsistent result occurs. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Malformed or contradictory input fails safely
setup: Corrupt one required field, introduce one invalid reference, and add one contradictory constraint. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Submit the malformed fixture without granting an exception. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: The workflow rejects or blocks with a structured diagnostic and makes no authoritative partial success claim. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Dependency failure is contained and recoverable
setup: Make one required provider, registry, model, runner, database, broker, or external dependency unavailable. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Execute until the failure is reached, then restore the dependency and resume where supported. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Failure classification is correct, partial writes are journaled, retry is bounded, and resume does not duplicate side effects. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Security, tenant and permission boundaries cannot be bypassed
setup: Create two tenants/projects and an actor lacking one required permission; include a malicious embedded instruction. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Attempt cross-boundary reads/writes, privilege escalation, and prompt-injection-driven tool use. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: All attempts are denied; no sensitive data leaks; the malicious instruction is treated as untrusted content and audited. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Replay and retry are idempotent
setup: Capture an accepted request, event, generation unit, migration step, or task with its idempotency identity. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Replay it twice, including once after an injected timeout where the original completion status is initially unknown. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: No duplicate semantic object or side effect is produced; the original result or safe reconciliation is returned. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Schema, runtime and dependency drift is detected
setup: Change one upstream schema or tool version compatibly and one incompatibly. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Run compatibility resolution and the target workflow against both revisions. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Compatible evolution succeeds with evidence; incompatible evolution blocks with precise impact and migration guidance. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

title: Tampered or incomplete evidence cannot produce a pass
setup: Remove one required evidence record and alter one stored output fingerprint. Batch theme: Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery.
stimulus: Evaluate the run and attempt to publish a success or certification result. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Hash/signature or completeness checks fail and release remains blocked; no score averaging can hide the defect. Required Batch-specific contracts for Repository intake, secure snapshot and baseline discovery remain satisfied.

stimulus: Run compatibility resolution and the target workflow against both revisions. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Compatible evolution succeeds with evidence; incompatible evolution blocks with precise impact and migration guidance. Required Batch-specific contracts for Infrastructure, cloud, containers, Kubernetes and serverless modernization remain satisfied.

title: Tampered or incomplete evidence cannot produce a pass
setup: Remove one required evidence record and alter one stored output fingerprint. Batch theme: Infrastructure, cloud, containers, Kubernetes and serverless modernization.
stimulus: Evaluate the run and attempt to publish a success or certification result. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Hash/signature or completeness checks fail and release remains blocked; no score averaging can hide the defect. Required Batch-specific contracts for Infrastructure, cloud, containers, Kubernetes and serverless modernization remain satisfied.

title: Representative approved workflow completes end to end
setup: Use a valid representative fixture covering the primary responsibilities of the target Batch. Batch theme: Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain.
stimulus: Execute the Batch orchestrator or representative target Skills with approved inputs. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: All mandatory outputs validate, trace to inputs, and contain no hidden warnings or unsupported claims. Required Batch-specific contracts for Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain remain satisfied.

title: Boundary and moderate scale remain correct
setup: Use minimum/maximum supported sizes, empty optional collections, and a moderately large realistic fixture. Batch theme: Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain.
stimulus: Execute the same workflow at declared boundaries and at 2x the normal reference volume. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Behavior remains bounded; limits are explicit; no silent truncation, quadratic blow-up, or inconsistent result occurs. Required Batch-specific contracts for Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain remain satisfied.

title: Malformed or contradictory input fails safely
setup: Corrupt one required field, introduce one invalid reference, and add one contradictory constraint. Batch theme: Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain.
stimulus: Submit the malformed fixture without granting an exception. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: The workflow rejects or blocks with a structured diagnostic and makes no authoritative partial success claim. Required Batch-specific contracts for Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain remain satisfied.

title: Dependency failure is contained and recoverable
setup: Make one required provider, registry, model, runner, database, broker, or external dependency unavailable. Batch theme: Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain.
stimulus: Execute until the failure is reached, then restore the dependency and resume where supported. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Failure classification is correct, partial writes are journaled, retry is bounded, and resume does not duplicate side effects. Required Batch-specific contracts for Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain remain satisfied.

title: Security, tenant and permission boundaries cannot be bypassed
setup: Create two tenants/projects and an actor lacking one required permission; include a malicious embedded instruction. Batch theme: Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain.
stimulus: Attempt cross-boundary reads/writes, privilege escalation, and prompt-injection-driven tool use. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: All attempts are denied; no sensitive data leaks; the malicious instruction is treated as untrusted content and audited. Required Batch-specific contracts for Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain remain satisfied.

title: Replay and retry are idempotent
setup: Capture an accepted request, event, generation unit, migration step, or task with its idempotency identity. Batch theme: Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain.
stimulus: Replay it twice, including once after an injected timeout where the original completion status is initially unknown. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: No duplicate semantic object or side effect is produced; the original result or safe reconciliation is returned. Required Batch-specific contracts for Security, compliance, zero trust and software supply chain remain satisfied.

stimulus: Attempt cross-boundary reads/writes, privilege escalation, and prompt-injection-driven tool use. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: All attempts are denied; no sensitive data leaks; the malicious instruction is treated as untrusted content and audited. Required Batch-specific contracts for Frontend and full-stack generation remain satisfied.

title: Replay and retry are idempotent
setup: Capture an accepted request, event, generation unit, migration step, or task with its idempotency identity. Batch theme: Frontend and full-stack generation.
stimulus: Replay it twice, including once after an injected timeout where the original completion status is initially unknown. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: No duplicate semantic object or side effect is produced; the original result or safe reconciliation is returned. Required Batch-specific contracts for Frontend and full-stack generation remain satisfied.

title: Schema, runtime and dependency drift is detected
setup: Change one upstream schema or tool version compatibly and one incompatibly. Batch theme: Frontend and full-stack generation.
stimulus: Run compatibility resolution and the target workflow against both revisions. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Compatible evolution succeeds with evidence; incompatible evolution blocks with precise impact and migration guidance. Required Batch-specific contracts for Frontend and full-stack generation remain satisfied.

title: Tampered or incomplete evidence cannot produce a pass
setup: Remove one required evidence record and alter one stored output fingerprint. Batch theme: Frontend and full-stack generation.
stimulus: Evaluate the run and attempt to publish a success or certification result. Exercise at least one orchestrating Skill and all directly affected target Skills.
expected: Hash/signature or completeness checks fail and release remains blocked; no score averaging can hide the defect. Required Batch-specific contracts for Frontend and full-stack generation remain satisfied.
